title: Gut Health Habits Checklist
description: Review these evidence-based habits that support your gut-brain connection. Check off each item you are currently following, then work on adding the unchecked ones over time.
instructions: Go through each item and honestly check off the ones you are consistently doing. The unchecked items become your improvement targets. Revisit this checklist weekly to track your progress.

items.label: I eat 20-30 different plant foods per week (fruits, vegetables, grains, nuts, seeds)
I include fermented foods regularly (yogurt, kefir, sauerkraut, kimchi, etc.)
I eat prebiotic-rich foods (garlic, onions, leeks, bananas, oats, asparagus)
I limit ultra-processed foods and artificial sweeteners
I drink 6-8 glasses of water daily
I include omega-3 rich foods (fatty fish, walnuts, flaxseeds) at least 2x per week
I eat colorful fruits and vegetables daily (aim for 5+ servings)
I limit added sugar to less than 25g (6 tsp) per day
I eat regular meals (not skipping breakfast or going long periods without food)
I eat slowly and mindfully (not while distracted by screens)
I stop eating 2-3 hours before bedtime
I practice stress management (the gut-brain axis is bidirectional)
I prioritize 7-9 hours of quality sleep (poor sleep disrupts gut bacteria)
I get regular physical activity (exercise improves gut microbial diversity)

disclaimer: This checklist is for educational purposes only. If you have dietary restrictions, food allergies, or an eating disorder, consult a healthcare provider before making changes. If you are in crisis, call or text 988.
